message:
style(tests,benches,conformance): apply rustfmt 2024 formatting to test suite

Apply Rust 2024 edition formatting across the full test and benchmark surface:

**Benchmarks** (14 files):
  cancel_drain_bench, cancel_trace_bench, egraph_benchmark, geodesic_benchmark,
  golden_output, homology_benchmark, methodology_baselines, phase0_baseline,
  protocol_benchmark, raptorq_benchmark, reactor_benchmark, scheduler_benchmark,
  spork_baseline, timer_wheel, tracing_overhead
  — Import reordering (`criterion::{...}` items sorted alphabetically),
    `std::sync::Arc` before `std::sync::atomic::...`

**Integration/e2e tests** (~80 files):
  All tests/ and tests/e2e/ files reformatted: import ordering, assert macro
  multi-line unwrapping, single-line if-else collapsing

**Conformance suite** (conformance/src/):
  bench/benchmarks.rs, bench/mod.rs, bench/runner.rs, lean_coverage_matrix.rs,
  lean_frontier.rs, lib.rs, runner.rs, tests/budget.rs, tests/cancellation.rs,
  tests/channels.rs, tests/io.rs, tests/negative.rs, tests/outcome.rs,
  tests/runtime.rs, traceability.rs

**Examples** (6 files):
  cancellation_injection, chaos_testing, demo_benchmark, demo_delta_debug,
  demo_record_nondeterministic, macros_nested

**franken_evidence** (src/lib.rs, src/render.rs, tests/comprehensive.rs)
**franken_decision** (src/lib.rs)

No functional changes — purely mechanical rustfmt 2024 reformatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 //! Lean coverage matrix schema validation tests (bd-13aa6).
 
 use conformance::{
-    CoverageRowType, CoverageStatus, LeanCoverageMatrix, LEAN_COVERAGE_SCHEMA_VERSION,
+    CoverageRowType, CoverageStatus, LEAN_COVERAGE_SCHEMA_VERSION, LeanCoverageMatrix,
 };
 use serde_json::Value;
 use std::collections::BTreeSet;
@@ -21,43 +21,59 @@ fn sample_matrix_parses_and_validates() {
 #[test]
 fn sample_matrix_contains_all_required_row_types() {
     let matrix = LeanCoverageMatrix::from_json_str(SAMPLE_JSON).expect("sample must parse");
-    assert!(matrix
-        .rows
-        .iter()
-        .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::SemanticRule));
-    assert!(matrix
-        .rows
-        .iter()
-        .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::Invariant));
-    assert!(matrix
-        .rows
-        .iter()
-        .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::RefinementObligation));
-    assert!(matrix
-        .rows
-        .iter()
-        .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::OperationalGate));
+    assert!(
+        matrix
+            .rows
+            .iter()
+            .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::SemanticRule)
+    );
+    assert!(
+        matrix
+            .rows
+            .iter()
+            .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::Invariant)
+    );
+    assert!(
+        matrix
+            .rows
+            .iter()
+            .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::RefinementObligation)
+    );
+    assert!(
+        matrix
+            .rows
+            .iter()
+            .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::OperationalGate)
+    );
 }
 
 #[test]
 fn sample_matrix_contains_status_model_examples() {
     let matrix = LeanCoverageMatrix::from_json_str(SAMPLE_JSON).expect("sample must parse");
-    assert!(matrix
-        .rows
-        .iter()
-        .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::InProgress));
-    assert!(matrix
-        .rows
-        .iter()
-        .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::Blocked));
-    assert!(matrix
-        .rows
-        .iter()
-        .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::Proven));
-    assert!(matrix
-        .rows
-        .iter()
-        .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::ValidatedInCi));
+    assert!(
+        matrix
+            .rows
+            .iter()
+            .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::InProgress)
+    );
+    assert!(
+        matrix
+            .rows
+            .iter()
+            .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::Blocked)
+    );
+    assert!(
+        matrix
+            .rows
+            .iter()
+            .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::Proven)
+    );
+    assert!(
+        matrix
+            .rows
+            .iter()
+            .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::ValidatedInCi)
+    );
 }
 
 #[test]

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Seeds for failure cases proptest has generated in the past. It is
+# automatically read and these particular cases re-run before any
+# novel cases are generated.
+#
+# It is recommended to check this file in to source control so that
+# everyone who runs the test benefits from these saved cases.
+cc 9da656f02667c79d0b79be037bcc88a75299dd81ffa42cc3d4ff4b0ba0a95028 # shrinks to cancel_req = 0, cancelling = 0, finalizing = 0, ready_depth = 0, obligations = 0, draining = 2, deadline = 9.249991382432674
